_comment: Follow-up question templates for Osler. Edit here, not in code.

neuro_screen._note: Neurologic localization — highest priority. Ask when numbness/weakness/tingling present.
numbness: Is the numbness on one side of your body (face, arm, or leg), or is it on both sides?
Did the numbness start suddenly, or come on gradually over minutes/hours?
Do you also have any weakness, difficulty speaking, or changes in your vision?
weakness: Is the weakness on one side of your body, or both sides?
Did it start suddenly or gradually?
tingling: Is the tingling on one side of your body or both sides, and where exactly?

dizziness: Is the dizziness more like lightheadedness/faintness, or a spinning sensation?
rash: Where is the rash? Is it spreading, raised, or itchy?
redness: Where is the redness? (skin, eyes, face, or a specific area)
swelling: Where is the swelling? (one leg, both legs, face, or all over)
joint pain: Is the joint pain in one joint or multiple joints, and which ones?

dehydration._note: Ask only when GI symptoms present OR dizziness WITHOUT neuro symptoms.
default: Have you been able to keep food and fluids down, or has nausea been preventing you from eating or drinking?
Are you urinating less than usual, or feeling very thirsty or dry-mouthed?

vestibular._note: Positional dizziness — ask when dizziness present without neuro symptoms.
default: Is the dizziness worse when you stand up quickly, turn your head, or change positions?
Have you had any fever, chills, or felt like you might be coming down with something?
Have you been around anyone who is sick, or traveled recently?

medication._note: Always ask when unexplained symptoms without clear cause.
default: Have you started any new medications, supplements, or changed your dosage recently?

gi: Is there any blood in your stool?
Have you had any severe abdominal pain or cramping?

high_priority._note: Forced questions for high-priority symptoms — override normal buckets.
chest pain: What does the chest pain feel like? (pressure/squeezing, sharp/stabbing, burning, or aching)
How long has the chest pain lasted? Is it constant or comes and goes?
Do you have shortness of breath, sweating, or lightheadedness with the chest pain?
Does the pain spread to your arm, jaw, neck, or back?
Does it get worse with physical activity or deep breathing?
shortness of breath: Did the shortness of breath come on suddenly or gradually?
Is it worse when lying down or with exertion?
numbness: Is the numbness on one side of your body, or both sides?
Where exactly is the numbness — face, arm, hand, leg, or a specific area?
Did the numbness start suddenly or come on gradually?
Do you also have any weakness, difficulty speaking, or vision changes?
weakness: Is the weakness on one side of your body or both sides?
Did it start suddenly or gradually?
Do you also have numbness, difficulty speaking, or vision changes?
palpitations: How long do the palpitations last — seconds, minutes, or longer?
Do you feel lightheadedness, near-fainting, or chest discomfort with them?
Do they start and stop suddenly, or gradually?

high_risk_safety._note: Shown instead of normal questions when high-risk freeze active.
chest pain: Can you describe the chest pain character? (pressure, sharp, burning)
Is it worse with exertion or breathing?
Any sweating, nausea, or shortness of breath?
shortness of breath: Did the shortness of breath come on suddenly?
Any chest pain or leg swelling?
Are you able to speak in full sentences?
syncope: Did you lose consciousness completely or just feel faint?
Any warning signs before it happened (palpitations, chest pain)?

suppress_patterns._note: Question substrings to suppress if already asked. Case-insensitive.
